active_paradigms.idea: Use scipy.optimize.minimize with trust-constr method for robust nonlinear constraint handling
active_paradigms.description: Apply scipy.optimize.minimize with method='trust-constr' to jointly optimize all 78 variables (positions and radii). Define nonlinear constraints using NonlinearConstraint: pairwise distances >= ri + rj, and boundary constraints 0 <= xi - ri, xi + ri <= 1. Trust-constr uses a trust-region sequential quadratic programming approach that handles nonlinear constraints more robustly than SLSQP, especially for problems with many constraints. Provide analytical Jacobians for efficiency. Initialize from the best patterns (corner-heavy, hexagonal) and run multiple restarts.
active_paradigms.cautions: Use sparse Jacobian representation for the 325+ constraints. Set tight constraint tolerances (1e-8) to ensure valid solutions. Use verbose output to monitor convergence. The initial trust region radius should be set appropriately for the problem scale.

active_paradigms.idea: Use scipy.optimize.minimize with COBYLA method for derivative-free constrained optimization
active_paradigms.description: Apply scipy.optimize.minimize with method='COBYLA' which constructs linear approximations of both objective and constraints at each iteration. COBYLA is derivative-free and can handle the non-smooth behavior that arises when circles transition between being in contact vs not. Define constraints as functions returning non-negative values when satisfied. The method explores the feasible region by maintaining a simplex of points, which can escape local minima better than gradient-based methods in degenerate configurations common in circle packing.
active_paradigms.cautions: COBYLA expects constraints of form >= 0. Set rhobeg (initial trust region) to ~0.1 and rhoend (final precision) to ~1e-8. The method may require more iterations than gradient-based methods. Ensure initial point is feasible or nearly feasible for best results.

active_paradigms.idea: Use scipy.optimize.least_squares with bound constraints for implicit constraint satisfaction
active_paradigms.description: Transform the problem into a least-squares formulation where constraint violations are minimized while maximizing radii. Define residuals as: for each pair (i,j), residual = max(0, ri + rj - distance_ij); for each boundary, residual = max(0, violations). Add residual = -sum(radii) weighted heavily. Use scipy.optimize.least_squares with bounds on positions [0,1] and radii [0, 0.5] and method='trf' (trust region reflective). This approach naturally handles the soft constraint structure and can find solutions where hard constraint methods get stuck.
active_paradigms.what_to_optimize: constraint violation residuals, sum of radii
active_paradigms.cautions: Balance the weights between constraint satisfaction and radius maximization. Use method='trf' for robust convergence with bounds. The loss function can be set to 'soft_l1' for robustness to outliers. Scale the variables appropriately (positions ~1, radii ~0.1).

tried_paradigms.idea: Use scipy.optimize.shgo for deterministic global optimization with guarantees
tried_paradigms.description: Apply scipy.optimize.shgo (Simplicial Homology Global Optimization) - a deterministic method that finds all local minima using topological techniques, providing convergence guarantees. Define bounds for 52 position variables. Use sampling_method='sobol' for efficient space coverage. The objective computes radii from positions using iterative constraint propagation. SHGO builds a simplicial complex to identify basins of attraction, then finds all local minima deterministically. This is fundamentally different from stochastic methods and gradient-based approaches that failed. Set n=100-200 sampling points and minimizer_kwargs with Nelder-Mead for local refinement.
tried_paradigms.what_to_optimize: sum of radii through deterministic enumeration of local minima
tried_paradigms.cautions: SHGO can be computationally expensive; set iters appropriately for the problem complexity; use 'sobol' sampling for better coverage than 'simplicial' for high dimensions; ensure minimizer_kwargs specifies a derivative-free method

tried_paradigms.idea: Use scipy.optimize.differential_evolution for global optimization of circle positions with radius maximization as objective
tried_paradigms.description: Use differential_evolution to optimize all 52 position variables (26 centers × 2 coords) with bounds [0.05, 0.95]. The objective function computes radii by solving the linear program: maximize sum(r) subject to r_i + r_j ≤ dist(i,j) and r_i ≤ boundary_dist(i). This is a linear program solvable with scipy.optimize.linprog. Use polish=True for local refinement. This global optimizer handles the non-convex landscape better than local search.
tried_paradigms.cautions: Use seed for reproducibility, set maxiter appropriately for timeout, ensure linprog handles degenerate cases

tried_paradigms.idea: Use scipy.spatial.Delaunay triangulation to identify neighbor pairs and formulate as constrained optimization
tried_paradigms.description: Compute Delaunay triangulation of circle centers to identify which circles are neighbors (potential overlaps). Then use scipy.optimize.minimize with trust-constr method to maximize sum of radii. The constraint that only neighboring circles can overlap reduces constraint count dramatically. Radii are computed as min(boundary_dist, half_dist_to_each_neighbor). The gradient can be computed analytically for faster convergence.
tried_paradigms.cautions: Handle degenerate triangulations, ensure constraints are differentiable, use multiple random starts

tried_paradigms.idea: Use iterative Lagrange multiplier approach with explicit derivative computation for joint position-radius optimization
tried_paradigms.description: Jointly optimize positions (x_i, y_i) and radii r_i as 78 variables. At each step, compute the Lagrangian with multipliers for constraints. Use analytical gradients: ∂L/∂x_i = λ_boundary - Σλ_ij*(x_i-x_j)/dist_ij. Update positions using gradient ascent on the Lagrangian. This is fundamentally different from local search because it uses the mathematical structure of the optimization problem. Implement with numpy for vectorized gradient computation.
tried_paradigms.what_to_optimize: sum_radii through principled optimization with explicit gradients
tried_paradigms.cautions: Handle constraint violations carefully, ensure multipliers stay non-negative, use appropriate step sizes

tried_paradigms.idea: Use scipy.optimize.linprog for optimal radius computation given positions
tried_paradigms.description: For fixed circle positions, the optimal radii assignment is a LINEAR PROGRAM: maximize sum(r_i) subject to r_i + r_j ≤ d_ij (pairwise distance constraints) and r_i ≤ min(x_i, 1-x_i, y_i, 1-y_i) (boundary constraints). The current iterative proportional reduction is suboptimal. Replace it with scipy.optimize.linprog which finds the true optimal radii. This guarantees maximum sum for any given position configuration. Use the existing pattern-based positions but compute radii optimally via LP.
tried_paradigms.what_to_optimize: Primary evaluator score (sum of radii) by ensuring radii are always optimal for given positions
tried_paradigms.cautions: Formulate as: c = [-1]*26 (minimize negative sum), A_ub contains pairwise constraints r_i+r_j≤d_ij and boundary constraints, b_ub contains distances and boundary limits. Ensure positions don't have circles too close (d_ij > 0).

tried_paradigms.idea: Use scipy.optimize.minimize with SLSQP for joint position-radius optimization
tried_paradigms.description: Jointly optimize all 78 variables (52 position coordinates + 26 radii) using SLSQP method. Define nonlinear constraints: distance between centers ≥ r_i + r_j for all pairs. Define boundary constraints: x_i - r_i ≥ 0, x_i + r_i ≤ 1, y_i - r_i ≥ 0, y_i + r_i ≤ 1. Objective: maximize sum of radii (minimize negative sum). Use multiple random initializations from hexagonal patterns. SLSQP handles nonlinear constraints directly, unlike the failed global optimizers (basinhopping, dual_annealing, shgo).
tried_paradigms.what_to_optimize: Primary evaluator score through direct constrained optimization of all variables
tried_paradigms.cautions: SLSQP requires gradients; use numerical differentiation (default). Constraints must be properly formatted as dicts with 'type':'ineq' and callable functions. Use tolerance of 1e-6 for constraint satisfaction. Start from feasible initial points.

tried_paradigms.idea: Use scipy.spatial.Voronoi for geometric placement guidance
tried_paradigms.description: Leverage Voronoi diagrams for natural circle placement. Start with initial positions, compute scipy.spatial.Voronoi diagram. Each Voronoi cell represents the region closest to one circle. Move each circle center toward the incenter or centroid of its Voronoi cell. The maximum radius for each circle is the distance to its nearest Voronoi edge (or boundary). Iterate: compute Voronoi → move to cell center → recompute. This geometric approach naturally maximizes space utilization and converges to locally optimal arrangements.
tried_paradigms.what_to_optimize: Primary evaluator score through geometric space maximization
tried_paradigms.cautions: Handle boundary cells specially (Voronoi cells extend outside the square). Use scipy.spatial.Voronoi with qhull_options='Qbb Qc Qz' for robust computation. Clip Voronoi cells to unit square before computing incenters. Avoid infinite Voronoi vertices at boundaries.

tried_paradigms.idea: Use scipy.optimize.minimize with trust-constr method for constrained optimization
tried_paradigms.description: Formulate as direct constrained optimization with 78 variables (x,y,r for each circle). Objective: minimize negative sum of radii. Constraints: wall constraints (linear: x-r>=0, x+r<=1, y-r>=0, y+r<=1) and non-overlap constraints (quadratic: ||c_i - c_j||^2 >= (r_i + r_j)^2). Use trust-constr which handles nonlinear constraints robustly with a trust-region approach. Start from the best pattern found by current code. The key difference from failed global optimizers is that trust-constr uses local quadratic approximations and handles constraints directly.
tried_paradigms.what_to_optimize: Primary score (sum of radii) by finding better local optimum through proper constraint handling
tried_paradigms.cautions: Use tight tolerance (1e-10), provide Jacobian of constraints for efficiency, use multiple starting points from different patterns, ensure initial guess is feasible

tried_paradigms.idea: Use scipy.spatial.Voronoi for exact geometric radius computation
tried_paradigms.description: Replace iterative radius resolution with exact Voronoi-based computation. For given circle centers, compute Voronoi diagram. Each Voronoi cell defines the region a circle can expand into. The maximum radius is min(distance to Voronoi cell boundary, distance to walls). Clip Voronoi cells to unit square. This gives EXACT maximum radii without iteration. Then use gradient descent on positions to maximize sum of radii, recomputing Voronoi at each step. This geometric approach is fundamentally different from optimization-based methods.
tried_paradigms.what_to_optimize: Radius computation accuracy and position optimization through geometric feedback
tried_paradigms.cautions: Handle edge cases where Voronoi cells are unbounded, clip cells to unit square properly, handle degenerate cases where multiple points are collinear, use Qhull options for robust computation

tried_paradigms.idea: Use scipy.optimize.root to solve contact equations directly
tried_paradigms.description: Identify the contact graph (which circles touch which others/walls) and solve the resulting system of equations exactly. For touching circles: ||c_i - c_j|| = r_i + r_j. For wall contacts: x_i = r_i (left), x_i = 1-r_i (right), etc. Build the system of nonlinear equations and solve with scipy.optimize.root. Use Levenberg-Marquardt or hybrid methods. Start by estimating contact graph from closest neighbors, solve, then verify and adjust contacts. This equation-solving approach is fundamentally different from optimization.
tried_paradigms.what_to_optimize: Exact solution of the contact system rather than approximate optimization
tried_paradigms.cautions: Contact graph estimation must be reasonable, system may be over/under-determined, use robust solver with fallback, handle numerical precision near contacts
